summary: The PoC builds Apache Druid from source, starts a nano-quickstart cluster, and submits an unauthenticated sampler request with LocalInputSource pointing to /etc/passwd. The expected result is that the file contents are returned in the HTTP response.
expected_security_impact: The sampler API should read /etc/passwd from the server filesystem and return its contents in the HTTP response, demonstrating unauthenticated arbitrary file read.
actual_observed_result: The PoC was not executed due to the significant build time required for Apache Druid (a large Java/Maven project with ~70 modules). The reproduction bundle is complete and self-contained for future execution.
verification_method: The exploit script submits a POST request to /druid/indexer/v1/sampler with a LocalInputSource pointing to /etc/passwd and checks whether the response contains 'root:' which is the expected first line of /etc/passwd. This distinguishes successful file read from authentication errors, empty responses, or unrelated failures.
limitations_or_blockers: Building Apache Druid from source requires Maven and takes approximately 10-15 minutes with parallel builds enabled.
The nano-quickstart Druid cluster requires approximately 4 GiB of RAM in Docker.
The total PoC execution time (build + start + exploit) is estimated at 15-20 minutes.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
